Package global.thalion.ttio.exporters

Class BamWriter

java.lang.Object
global.thalion.ttio.exporters.BamWriter
Direct Known Subclasses:
CramWriter
public class BamWriter extends Object
BAM exporter — M88.

Writes a WrittenGenomicRun to BAM by formatting the in- memory parallel-array representation as SAM text and piping that text via stdin to the user-installed samtools binary (samtools view -bS -, optionally piped through samtools sort -O bam). Subprocess-only — no htslib linkage; SAM line layout follows the public SAMv1 specification.

Quality byte encoding

M87's BamReader stores SAM's QUAL field bytes verbatim into WrittenGenomicRun.qualities() — i.e. the buffer holds ASCII Phred+33 characters (so a Phred-40 score is stored as the byte value 73, the ASCII code for 'I'). This writer mirrors that convention: each qualities[i] byte is written directly as the SAM QUAL character with no arithmetic adjustment. The pair is therefore lossless byte-for-byte across the M87 read / M88 write round trip.

Cross-language note: the Python and Objective-C implementations adopt the same convention so cross-language conformance dumps match.

Cross-language equivalents: Python ttio.exporters.bam.BamWriter, Objective-C TTIOBamWriter.

(M88)

  • Field Details

    • DEFAULT_SQ_LENGTH

      protected static final long DEFAULT_SQ_LENGTH
      Default @SQ LN: length when the writer doesn't know the true reference length. SAM requires LN: on every @SQ; we pick INT32_MAX so the emitted header is valid for any plausible coordinate. Matches the Python reference's _DEFAULT_SQ_LENGTH and the ObjC writer's constant for cross-language byte-equality on the unsorted code path.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

  • Constructor Details

    • BamWriter

      public BamWriter(Path path)
      Construct a BamWriter.
      Parameters:
      path - output BAM file path. The .bam extension is honoured by samtools' file-format auto-detection (HANDOFF Gotcha §165).

  • Method Details

    • path

      public Path path()
      Returns:
      the output path passed at construction time.

    • write

      public void write(WrittenGenomicRun run, List<ProvenanceRecord> provenance, boolean sort) throws IOException
      Serialise run to the configured output path.
      Parameters:
      run - the genomic-run container to write.
      provenance - optional provenance records to inject as @PG header lines. Pass an empty list for none; the M87/M88 cross-language convention is "writer accepts provenance explicitly because the Java/ObjC WrittenGenomicRun analogues don't carry it".
      sort - when true (Binding Decision §137 default), pipe the SAM text through samtools sort -O bam so the output BAM is coordinate-sorted (precondition most downstream tools expect — IGV, GATK, samtools index). When false, output is written in input read order and @HD SO: is set to unsorted.
      Throws:
      IOException - if samtools is missing, exits non-zero, or a piping I/O error occurs.

    • buildViewCommand

      protected List<String> buildViewCommand(boolean sort)
      Build the samtools view stage of the pipeline. When sort is true, the view stage emits to stdout (the sort stage consumes from stdin); when false, the view stage writes directly to path.

      Subclasses override to swap in CRAM flags.

    • buildSortCommand

      protected List<String> buildSortCommand(boolean sort)
      Build the samtools sort stage of the pipeline, or null for the unsorted single-stage path.
